channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's Government Strengthens Cooperatives for Economic Growth
item.description: Tanzania's government has pledged to accelerate reforms in the cooperative sector, positioning it as a cornerstone of the nation's economic transformation strategy. The reforms include enhancing gover...

item.title: Lagos government warns against spreading misleading flood images
item.description: The Lagos State Government has accused social media influencers and bloggers of circulating misleading flood images from other locations and falsely attributing them to Lagos. Commissioner Tokunbo Wah...

item.title: France Advances to Last Eight After Narrow Win Over Paraguay, Morocco Defeats Canada in World Cup 2026
item.description: In a dramatic World Cup 2026 clash, France secured a 1-0 victory over Paraguay in Philadelphia, with Kylian Mbappé converting a penalty in the 70th minute to seal the win. The match, played under extr...

item.title: Gold Theft in Sivakasi: Masked Robbers Break Into Businessman's Home
item.description: Three masked men stole over 90 sovereigns of gold from the residence of businessman Sri Aravind Ram in Sivakasi on Saturday night. The family returned home around 11:30 p.m. to find the main gate brok...

item.title: Americans Opt for Regular Gasoline to Save Costs Amid High Prices
item.description: A growing number of U.S. drivers are switching from premium gasoline to regular to save money, despite the latter being less optimal for high-performance vehicles. Data from fuel cash-back app Upside ...

item.title: Harry Styles concludes historic 12-night Wembley residency with Pride celebration and special appearance by Gemma Styles
item.description: Harry Styles brought the London leg of his 'Together, Together' residency tour to a close on 4 July with the twelfth and final performance at Wembley Stadium, setting a new record for the most shows p...

item.title: Australia wins T20 Women's World Cup against England
item.description: Australia secured their seventh T20 Women's World Cup title with a commanding seven-wicket victory over England at a sold-out Lord's. The final showcased a one-sided performance, with Australia's domi...

item.title: Alex Eala to Compete in Wimbledon Round of 16 at PhilSports Arena on July 6, 2026
item.description: The article announces a free public viewing event for Filipino tennis star Alex Eala's Wimbledon Round of 16 match against Jasmine Paolini, scheduled for July 6, 2026, at the PhilSports Arena in Pasig...

item.title: Nueva Vizcaya grants ₱50,000 incentive to couples married for 50 years or more
item.description: The provincial government of Nueva Vizcaya has awarded ₱50,000 each to at least 200 couples who have reached 50 years of marriage, as part of its “Enduring Devotion” program. The initiative aims to re...

item.title: Alien Sound Festival Postponed Due to Weather-Related Stage Collapse
item.description: The Alien Sound Festival in Naples, Italy, has been postponed following the collapse of its main stage during setup due to strong winds. The event, scheduled for July 4-5 at the former NATO base in Ba...

item.title: Excommunicated Catholic splinter group says it hopes for future reconciliation with the Vatican
item.description: A priest from the Society of St Pius X (SSPX), a traditionalist Catholic splinter group, has expressed confidence that the organisation will eventually be reconciled with the Roman Catholic Church und...

item.title: Quezon Wins Multiple Awards at 9th Eddys
item.description: The film 'Quezon' emerged as a major winner at the 9th Eddys, claiming several prestigious awards including Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Actor. The ceremony, held on July 6, 2026, celebrated ...

item.title: Teacher and Carpenter Acquitted in Murder Case
item.description: Two Tanzanian professionals, a teacher and a carpenter, were acquitted in a high-profile murder case after the court found insufficient evidence to support the prosecution's claims. The case, which ga...

item.title: Mallory McMorrow exits Michigan Democratic Senate primary, narrowing race between Stevens and El-Sayed
item.description: Michigan State Senator Mallory McMorrow has withdrawn from the Democratic primary race for the U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, significantly reshaping the contest ahead of the general election campaign ...

item.title: Wallabies' Ryan Lonergan undergoes scans after injury in Ireland clash
item.description: Ryan Lonergan, the Brumbies' scrumhalf, underwent precautionary throat scans following a 33-31 loss to Ireland, despite delivering a standout performance in his first start for the Wallabies. The 28-y...

item.title: PDP Faction Calls for Investigation into Gbajabiamila Over Fake Agency Allegations
item.description: The article discusses a political scandal involving the PDP faction's demand for the ouster of Chief of Staff to the President, Adeyemi Gbajabiamila, over allegations of a fake government agency. The ...

item.title: Simba Sports Club Celebrates Historic Cup Victory with Massive Parade
item.description: Thousands of Simba Sports Club supporters transformed Dar es Salaam into a sea of red and white as they celebrated their team's CRDB Federation Cup victory with a massive parade. The celebration follo...

item.title: Ayesha Bajeta shares behind-the-scenes photos with the cast of “The Alibi”
item.description: The article highlights a collection of behind-the-scenes photos shared by Ayesha Bajeta during the production of the television series “The Alibi.” Based on the available information, the feature focu...

item.title: Tonia Mishiali Explores Immigration, Patriarchy, and Female Resilience in ‘The Lion at My Back’
item.description: Cypriot filmmaker Tonia Mishiali’s second feature film, “The Lion at My Back,” is featured in the Crystal Globe competition at the Karlovy Vary Film Festival and centers on intersecting stories of imm...

item.title: Millions in Jewelry Stolen from French Glassmaker Museum
item.description: A high-value theft occurred at a museum belonging to a French glassmaker, where millions of dollars worth of jewelry were stolen. The incident, reported on July 5, 2026, has led to the charging of two...
